content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Government Code Section 82039                    Date : January 12, 1979




From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
        Rich Eichman

Subject: Volunteer Lobbying


Facts:

• A registered lobbyist becomes ill and cannot perform his/her
  lobbying duties.

• Other registered lobbyists volunteer to lobby on behalf of
  the ill lobbyist.

• These lobbyists will not receive compensation from the ill
  lobbyist or his/her employer.


Question:

        What is the reporting obligation of a lobbyist who performs
        volunteer lobbying on behalf of the ill lobbyist?

Response:

• The lobbyist is required to disclose on his/her Lobbyist
  Report all legislative or administrative action he/she
  attempts to influence on behalf of the ill lobbyist.

• The lobbyist is required to disclose all payments made in
  connection with lobbying activity on behalf of the ill
  lobbyist.

• The lobbyist is not required to amend his/her registration
  statement or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer on his/her
  Lobbyist Report.

sections.question:

sections.conclusion: The lobbyist is required to disclose on his/her Lobbyist Report all legislative or administrative action he/she attempts to influence on behalf of the ill lobbyist. The lobbyist is required to disclose all payments made in connection with lobbying activity on behalf of the ill lobbyist. The lobbyist is not required to amend his/her registration statement or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer on his/her Lobbyist Report.
sections.facts:
• 
A registered lobbyist becomes ill and cannot perform his/her 
lobbying duties. 
• 
Other registered lobbyists volunteer to lobby on behalf of 
the ill lobbyist. 
• 
These lobbyists will not receive compensation from the ill 
lobbyist or his/her employer.
sections.question_synthetic: A registered lobbyist asks what reporting obligations apply when other registered lobbyists volunteer to perform lobbying duties on behalf of an ill lobbyist without compensation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Volunteer lobbyists must disclose all legislative or administrative actions they attempt to influence and any payments related to the volunteer lobbying on their Lobbyist Report, but they are not required to amend their registration or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC memorandum response with clear Question and Response sections. Uses "Response" rather than "Conclusion" but functions as advice letter format. No ambiguities in extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What is the reporting obligation of a lobbyist who performs 
volunteer lobbying on behalf of the ill lobbyist? 
Response: 
• 
The lobbyist is required to disclose on his/her Lobbyist 
Report all legislative or administrative action he/she 
attempts to influence on behalf of the ill lobbyist. 
• 
The lobbyist is required to disclose all payments made ln 
connection with lobbying activity on behalf of the ill 
lobbyist. 
• 
The lobbyist is not required to amend his/her registration 
statement or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer on his/her 
Lobbyist Report.

CONCLUSION: The lobbyist is required to disclose on his/her Lobbyist Report all legislative or administrative action he/she attempts to influence on behalf of the ill lobbyist. The lobbyist is required to disclose all payments made in connection with lobbying activity on behalf of the ill lobbyist. The lobbyist is not required to amend his/her registration statement or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer on his/her Lobbyist Report.
embedding.first_500_words: State of California Memorandum To : Government Code Section 82039 Date : January 12, 1979 From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION Rich Eichman Subject: Volunteer Lobbying Facts: • A registered lobbyist becomes ill and cannot perform his/her lobbying duties. • Other registered lobbyists volunteer to lobby on behalf of the ill lobbyist. • These lobbyists will not receive compensation from the ill lobbyist or his/her employer. Question: What is the reporting obligation of a lobbyist who performs volunteer lobbying on behalf of the ill lobbyist? Response: • The lobbyist is required to disclose on his/her Lobbyist Report all legislative or administrative action he/she attempts to influence on behalf of the ill lobbyist. • The lobbyist is required to disclose all payments made in connection with lobbying activity on behalf of the ill lobbyist. • The lobbyist is not required to amend his/her registration statement or disclose the ill lobbyist's employer on his/her Lobbyist Report.
embedding.summary: The FPPC addresses the reporting obligations of registered lobbyists who volunteer to perform lobbying duties on behalf of an ill lobbyist without compensation. Volunteer lobbyists must disclose their lobbying activities and related payments but need not amend registration or identify the ill lobbyist's employer.
